ddls:
CREATE TABLE "api_queries" (
  "query_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API query */,
  "query_date" TEXT /* Date when the query was made */,
  "query_hour" TEXT /* Time when the query was made */,
  "needed_time_millisec" INTEGER /* Time taken to process the query in milliseconds */,
  "response_length" TEXT /* Length of the response in bytes or error message */,
  "response_status" TEXT /* Status of the response (e.g., SUCCESS, ERROR, FAILED) */,
  "query_string" TEXT /* Full query string including parameters */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API endpoint */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* Information about the device or platform making the API request */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the device making the API request */,
  "query_params" TEXT /* Additional parameters passed in the query string */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("query_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_queries_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES api_endpoints ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each station */,
  "station_code" TEXT /* Code of the station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the station */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the station */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the station */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "query_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "query_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API query */,
  "source_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source station */,
  "destination_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the destination station */,
  "query_date" TEXT /* Date when the query was made */,
  "query_time" TEXT /* Time when the query was made */,
  "needed_time_millisec" INTEGER /* Time taken to process the query in milliseconds */,
  "response_length" TEXT /* Length of the response in bytes or error message */,
  "response_status" TEXT /* Status of the response (e.g., SUCCESS, ERROR, FAILED) */,
  "query_string" TEXT /* Full query string including parameters */,
  "query_params" TEXT /* Additional parameters passed in the query string */,
  "response_body" TEXT /* Actual response body for further analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_query_logs_destination_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("destination_station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_query_logs_source_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_query_logs_query_id FOREIGN KEY ("query_id") REFERENCES api_queries ("query_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for secure authentication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, admin, analyst) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the creation of the user account */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_queries" (
  "user_query_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user query entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "query_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API query */,
  "query_date" TEXT /* Date when the query was made */,
  "query_time" TEXT /* Time when the query was made */,
  "query_params" TEXT /* Additional parameters passed in the query string */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_query_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_queries_query_id FOREIGN KEY ("query_id") REFERENCES api_queries ("query_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_queries_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_types" (
  "response_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response type */,
  "response_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the response type (e.g., SUCCESS, ERROR, FAILED) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the response type */,
  "is_success" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the response type is a success or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API endpoint */,
  "endpoint_name" TEXT /* Name of the API endpoint */,
  "description" TEXT /* Short description of the API endpoint */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the API endpoint */,
  "method" TEXT /* HTTP method supported by the endpoint (e.g., GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) */,
  "description_long" TEXT /* Longer description of the API endpoint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id")
)
